Cynanchum nitida, commonly known as the 'Twining Trumpet Vine', is a climbing plant that belongs to the Apocynaceae family. 

The health benefits of Cynanchum nitida are largely attributed to its use in traditional medicine, where it has been employed to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. However, its efficacy and safety as a medicinal plant require further scientific validation.

Cynanchum nitida is reported to contain bioactive constituents such as alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with Cynanchum nitida include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, although these preparations are not widely recognized or standardized in modern herbalism.
